notes: Lebanese spoken Arabic should match Levantine Arabic for everyday travel comfort. English and French are also practically useful in Lebanon.

evidence.title: Glottolog reference linking Lebanese Arabic to North Levantine Spoken [apc]
evidence.note: Supports mapping Lebanese spoken Arabic to the app language code apc.

evidence.title: Britannica: Lebanon
evidence.note: Britannica describes Arabic as the official language and French and English as widely spoken.

notes: Aruba should score highly for English usability in traveler reality, not just official-language baseline.

evidence.title: Aruba.com official travel guide
evidence.note: Official tourism copy says most Arubans also speak English and Spanish and are happy to help guests in English.

evidence.title: Aruba.com Papiamento page
evidence.note: Official tourism page confirms Papiamento and Dutch as official languages.

notes: Andorra should not behave like a Catalan-only destination. Spanish and French are very present; English is useful in tourism but not as universal as Spanish or French.

evidence.title: Visit Andorra essential information
evidence.note: Official tourism site says Catalan is official and Spanish, French, and Portuguese are very present.

evidence.title: Visit Andorra English-language site
evidence.note: English inclusion is a tourism-utility override rather than an official-language claim.
